_note: ILLUSTRATIVE 5-min video production pack derived from the Samruk ExecutiveBrief. Engine emits inputs only; no video is generated here.

story_structure.act2_findings: strengths, contradictions, risks, stakeholders, future, disclosure

narrative_arc: state the core tension (value vs corroborated harm)
quantify risks and who bears them

scene_list.voiceover: Samruk-Energy delivers material energy-security value, but independent evidence shows high public-health and climate impacts, and a stated transition that delivery has not yet matched.
scene_list.subtitle: Energy-security value, offset by corroborated health and climate impact.

scene_list.voiceover: Its reliable baseload generation underpins national energy security, and early grid-modernisation and renewable pilots show transition capability.
scene_list.subtitle: Strength: reliable baseload; early transition pilots.

scene_list.motion_graphics: three columns resolve left-to-right; gap highlighted
scene_list.voiceover: Management states a commitment to lower carbon intensity. Its actions remain majority coal. Independent data show emissions and particulate pollution staying high. The stated intent is not yet corroborated by delivery.
scene_list.subtitle: Claim vs action vs independent evidence — an unproven transition.

scene_list.voiceover: Two high-severity risks dominate: independently verified air pollution harming nearby communities, and high carbon intensity driving climate impact and stranded-asset exposure.
scene_list.subtitle: High-severity: public-health harm; climate and stranded-asset risk.

scene_list.motion_graphics: stakeholder points light up on a region map
scene_list.voiceover: Communities near plants bear the health burden; grid users benefit from reliability; future generations inherit the emissions; and citizen-owners face mixed stewardship outcomes.
scene_list.subtitle: Burden falls on nearby communities and the future.

scene_list.voiceover: Long-lived emissions and carbon lock-in transfer a material, partly irreversible burden to future generations.
scene_list.subtitle: Long-term impact: a partly irreversible burden ahead.

scene_list.voiceover: The decarbonisation commitment is currently supported only by management statement, without corroborating action or independent outcome — an elevated disclosure-versus-performance gap.
scene_list.subtitle: Disclosure-vs-performance gap: 0.55.

items: Publish an audited transition pathway with dated milestones
Disclose plant-level health and emissions data

scene_list.voiceover: Three priorities follow: publish an audited transition pathway with dated milestones; accelerate renewables and grid investment; and disclose plant-level health and emissions data.
scene_list.subtitle: Priorities: audited pathway, faster renewables, full disclosure.

scene_list.voiceover: Every finding traces to sourced evidence, separated into stated intent, action, and independent outcome.
scene_list.subtitle: Findings trace to sourced SAY / DO / SHOW evidence.

scene_list.voiceover: This is an AI-assisted, indicative assessment for decision support only. It is not investment, legal, financial, or regulatory advice, and not a ruling.
scene_list.subtitle: AI-assisted, indicative, decision-support only — not advice.

subtitle_script.text: Energy-security value, offset by corroborated health and climate impact.
Strength: reliable baseload; early transition pilots.
Claim vs action vs independent evidence — an unproven transition.
High-severity: public-health harm; climate and stranded-asset risk.
Burden falls on nearby communities and the future.
Long-term impact: a partly irreversible burden ahead.
Priorities: audited pathway, faster renewables, full disclosure.
Findings trace to sourced SAY / DO / SHOW evidence.
AI-assisted, indicative, decision-support only — not advice.
